notes: A1 split (operator review): the precise kernel positivity hypothesis A2/A3 use, in the SHELL mass m_sh^2 (not mu^2/r). T6 PROVED CONDITIONAL APPROVED 2026-06-23 (operator). A2/A3 re-link here.
title: Scalar analytic branch: K>=m_sh^2>0 (the A2/A3 hypothesis)
statement: Assume Y>0, Z<0, m_sh^2>0. Then K(q)=m_sh^2+Y(|q|^2-q_star^2)^2 >= m_sh^2 > 0 for all q, and on a fixed periodic cell lambda0(L)=min_k K(k) >= m_sh^2 > 0 (equality only if a lattice mode lies exactly on the shell). This is the precise positivity input of A2 (sectoriality) and A3 (IR finiteness).
scope: scalar Brazovskii kernel; stated in the SHELL mass m_sh^2 (= old mu^2 = K(q_star)), NOT the zero-momentum r=K(0) nor the solver's mu2=r. Production-config certification is A1-PRODUCTION-KERNEL-MANIFEST (separate).
falsifier: A kernel with Y>0,Z<0,m_sh^2>0 yet inf_q K < m_sh^2, or lambda0(L) < m_sh^2.
no_overclaim: m_sh^2>0 (shell), NOT the solver mu2=r (zero momentum); no production-config certification; positivity only (not the dynamics/renormalisation themselves -- those are A2/A3).
next_action: Operator sign-off; A2 and A3 depend on this card (re-linked 2026-06-23).
